text:
Once upon a time, in the small town of Woodland Hollow, everyone had pet goldfish. Except for Fred. Fred didn’t have any fish—or even water. Fred lived in a cozy nook in the library's basement.

Every weekend, Fred would sit there, dreaming of a fishy adventure. His favorite thing about goldfish was their eternal optimism. You’d think they’d know they’d never escape that tank!

A quirky old bookshop owner decided to buy Fred from a discount bin. She’d never kept pets—she had a fear of things diving in unknown ways—but she liked Fred. A lot. Fred had a tiny aquarium she’d fill with pretty rocks and little green leaves.

Fred loved it. Every Saturday was a treasure hunt—a game of seek-and-find within his tiny kingdom.

One day, the bookshop owner decided Fred was missing a leaf. You knew Fred’s garden. It was like a fantasy land beneath her rock collection. She bought another leaf and mailed it
